spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Momentary, T: Individual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Perdo Animal Spells > LEVEL 20 > Blunt the Viper's Fangs
spells.description: Blunt the claws, teeth, talons, or other natural weapons of a beast. Only sharp weapons may be affected: this spell does not affect a weapon such as a horse's hoof or a giant snake's coils. The blunted weapon has its total damage (including Attack Advantage) reduced to half (round up). The beast may recover from the effect as if it were a Light Wound. The spell may affect animals of size up to +4.

spells.name: The Falcon's Hood
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Perdo Animal Spells > LEVEL 25 > The Falcon's Hood
spells.description: Destroys an animal's vision. The blindness heals as if it is a Medium Wound. The title refers to the hood used to cover a falcon's eyes when it is not hunting. When cast by Asaron of Flambeau, this spell causes the animals it targets to bear a faint silhouette of a flame in their eves.

spells.name: Cripple the Howling Wolf
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Perdo Animal Spells > LEVEL 25 > Cripple the Howling Wolf
spells.description: When casting this spell, you commonly make an abrupt snapping motion with your hands. One of the target's legs breaks cleanly. The animal cannot walk on that leg, but the damage heals as a Medium Wound. Note that the target need not be a wolf, and that you cannot choose which leg.

spells.name: Ward against the Beasts of Legend
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Ring, T: Circle
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > GENERAL > Ward against the Beasts of Legend
spells.description: No magical beast whose Magic Might is equal to or less than the level can affect anyone within the Circle or, if it is within the Circle, affect those outside. When a magus is making this and most other wards, a common gesture employed is the Fig Sign. The magus balls his or her right hand into a fist, placing the thumb between the index and middle fingers. A magus traveling to Italy should be warned that this sign is a deadly insult in that country.

spells.name: Disguise of the Putrid Aroma
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 5 > Disguise of the Putrid Aroma
spells.description: The target takes no interest in you as long as you continue to concentrate, and do not threaten it. A carnivore treats you as if you were simply something it does not eat. It retains normal interest, however, in anyone else nearby.

spells.name: Circle of Beast Warding
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Ring, T: Circle
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 5 > Circle of Beast Warding
spells.description: The caster inscribes a circle that no normal beast will cross.

spells.name: Soothe the Ferocious Bear
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 10 > Soothe the Ferocious Bear
spells.description: Calms an animal until it is threatened or aroused again. You must speak soothingly or musically to it, and something as simple as approaching it may arouse it again.

spells.name: Panic of the Elephant's Mouse
spells.parameter_line: R: Eye, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 15 > Panic of the Elephant's Mouse
spells.description: Makes an animal afraid of the target by preying upon its instinctive fears, causing it to attempt escape by the quickest, most feasible means possible. The animal may make a Size stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 to resist. (The title refers to the elephant's legendary fear of mice, but note that this spell cannot actually affect elephants, as they are too large.)

spells.name: Viper's Gaze
spells.parameter_line: R: Eye, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 15 > Viper's Gaze
spells.description: Holds an animal rigid as long as you maintain eye contact with it and concentrate on it.

spells.name: The Gentle Beast
spells.parameter_line: R: Eye, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 25 > The Gentle Beast
spells.description: Calms an animal, making it nearly fearless. It does not resist being ridden, shaved, or whatever else you can think of. If the animal enters combat, is extremely frightened, or incurs any kind of wound, the spell ends. The animal must be Size +4 or less.

spells.name: Mastering the Unruly Beast
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 25 > Mastering the Unruly Beast
spells.description: You can make an animal perform any act it is capable of. You can only issue new commands while touching the animal, but the animal will carry them out without requiring constant contact. Further, the animal will allow you to touch it throughout the duration of the spell. It is difficult to maintain control of particularly stubborn or fierce animals, such as mules or boars — creatures with appropriate Personality Traits may roll against an Ease Factor of 12 each round. When Chavin of Tytalus casts this spell, the creature acts surly and unfriendly while going about its tasks, even when Chavin doesn't specifically tell it to act that way.

spells.name: Commanding the Harnessed Beast
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Animal Spells > Rego Animal Spells > LEVEL 30 > Commanding the Harnessed Beast
spells.description:
You implant a complicated command into an animal, which it carries out to the best of its abilities. The command must involve completing a certain task, such as finding a certain person. Vague orders, like "protect me," do not work. If the animal does not complete the task before the end of the spell approaches, it becomes desperately ferocious, especially at night.

(Base 15, +1 Touch, +2 Sun)
---

spells.name: Footsteps of Slippery Oil
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Diam, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Creo Aquam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Footsteps of Slippery Oil
spells.description: From each of the next ten footsteps the caster of this spell takes, nonflammable grease spreads out, leaving a slick area 10 paces wide. Dexterity stress rolls are required to remain upright whenever a character on the oil does something more challenging than walking. A roll against an Ease Factor of 6 is needed for the average maneuver, with Ease Factors of up to 15 needed to (for example) make a right turn at full speed on a smooth, greased surface. The duration is the length of time that the oil lasts. This spell was invented by a rather good-natured magus, and the gestures associated with the most common variations of the spell are as comical as the effects themselves. You stand first on one leg, and then the other, vigorously rubbing the bottom of each upturned foot, as if spreading on a salve.

spells.name: Creeping Oil
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Creo Aquam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Creeping Oil
spells.description: Saturates a porous material with flammable oil. When casting this spell, you make a flicking motion with your index finger and thumb, as if propelling something at the target. A dot of oil appears on the target after the spell is finished, and slowly spreads over the target, saturating enough cloth to cover a person. If used on a person's clothes and ignited, the oil does +12 damage the first round, +6 the second, and +0 the third.

spells.name: Mighty Torrent of Water
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Creo Aquam Spells > LEVEL 20 > Mighty Torrent of Water
spells.description: A 3-foot-diameter jet of water sprays from your outstretched arms towards your target. The target of this gusher of water takes +10 damage and must make a Strength + Size stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 or be sent flying back. Marbaid of Flambeau's wizard's sigil is connected to his obsession with blood. In his version of the spell the target is hit by a gush of blood, and the stains do not go away until sunrise/sunset. This does not require a requisite, as it is a purely cosmetic effect.

spells.name: Deluge of Rushing and Dashing
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 10, +2 Voice, +1 Concentration, +3 size, so that the whole stream floods
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Creo Aquam Spells > Deluge of Rushing and Dashing
spells.description: Causes a stream to flood, starting at the stream's nearest point to you and continuing downstream. The results of this spell are generally quite impressive. As the stream overflows its original bed, it becomes a raging torrent, sucking in everything near it. Damage from the spell can last for many years. As long as you concentrate, the stream remains at flood levels. Anyone caught near the stream when it floods is struck by the rush of water (+10 damage), caught up in the flood, and dragged along. Every round, a victim makes a Strength + Swim stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9. Success means the character escapes the flood. Otherwise, the character loses one Fatigue level and takes +5 damage. Any who fall unconscious drown. This roll is repeated until the character either escapes the flood or dies.

spells.name: Clear Sight of the Naiad
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Mom, T: Vision
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Intellego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Clear Sight of the Naiad
spells.description: You can see as clearly through water as through air, for a single moment.

spells.name: Subtle Taste of Poison and Purity
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Conc, T: Taste
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Intellego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Subtle Taste of Poison and Purity
spells.description: You can taste all of the properties of any liquid or mixture of liquids. Unlike *Touch of the Pearls*, you can use this sense on any number of liquids during the duration of the spell, but you must taste the liquids in question.

spells.name: Touch of the Pearls
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Intellego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Touch of the Pearls
spells.description: Tells you whether a liquid you hold or touch is poisonous, just as a pearl sometimes does. Safer than Subtle Taste of Poison and Purity, as you don't have to taste the possibly poisonous liquid. In Verdan of Ex Miscellanea's version of the spell, poisonous liquids turn a faint green. According to his sigil, all his spells involve the color green.

spells.name: Call of the Rushing Waters
spells.parameter_line: R: Arc, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Intellego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Call of the Rushing Waters
spells.description: Allows you to find any natural body of water for which you have an Arcane Connection (for example, a vial of water from it). Once you cast the spell, you can hear the water while you concentrate. Following the sound eventually brings you to the water, but you must make simple Perception rolls against an Ease Factor of 6 to follow the sound accurately (roll once per day for long journeys). If you break concentration, you must cast the spell again. A body of water that does not move or that makes little noise requires a roll against an Ease Factor of 9 to be traced.

spells.name: Voice of the Lake
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Intellego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 25 > Voice of the Lake
spells.description: You can hold a conversation with a body of water. A body of water usually knows about things directly in contact with it, such as boats and fish. Genuine lakes are too complex for this spell to affect; they are not Individuals of Aquam.

spells.name: Enchantment of the Scrying Pool
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Year, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Intellego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 30 > Enchantment of the Scrying Pool
spells.description: Turns a body of water into a scrying pool. Innately magical people (including most companions with mystical powers) who look into the pool can see any spot they know of that is within sight of some natural body of water. The view you get is the same view that someone would have from a low boat on the water either close to shore or far from shore, as desired. Maximum range for scrying is 500 miles; the range of the spell is the range to the pool when the spell is cast. This ritual is not entirely of Hermetic magic, and does not conform to the normal InAq guidelines. It is an old Mercurian ritual that has become known throughout the Order, and which appears to use one body of water as an Arcane Connection to any other body of water. A number of theoreticians would like to make that work more generally, but so far this has not proved possible.

spells.name: Incantation of Putrid Wine
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Room
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Muto Aquam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Incantation of Putrid Wine
spells.description: Makes all liquids within the target room vile, and malodorous. The room where this spell is cast may not lose the stench for weeks or months. Note that this spell only affects liquids that are in the room when it is cast, and they remain malodorous even if removed from the room.

spells.name: Lungs of the Fish
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Muto Aquam Spells > LEVEL 20 > Lungs of the Fish
spells.description: Turns water into air as it enters your lungs, allowing you to breathe water as you do air. This spell puzzles Hermetic theoreticians, as it behaves like a dynamic container target despite not being a container target. All known attempts to create analogous effects have failed, even though analysis of this spell has not revealed any sign of non-Hermetic influence, or integrated experimentation. Most magi just point to it as an example of how Hermetic magic is not fully understood, and get on with their lives.

spells.name: Vile Water of Sterility
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Year, T: Group, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 4 (a very unnatural liquid), +1 Touch, +4 Year, +2 Group, +2 size
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Muto Aquam Spells > Vile Water of Sterility
spells.description: Ruins a body of water up to the size of a small lake, making it unfit for natural habitation and consumption. The water becomes dark and murky, and noxious fumes emanate from it. Vegetation on the banks either dies or becomes corrupt. Nearby trees become gnarled and blackened, and land animals flee the area.

spells.name: Comfort Of The Drenched Traveler
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Perdo Aquam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Comfort Of The Drenched Traveler
spells.description: Dries a person and his or her clothes.

spells.name: Parching Wind
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Perdo Aquam Spells > LEVEL 20 > Parching Wind
spells.description: Removes most of the water from any object, including plants (with a Herbam requisite), possibly making the object brittle and fragile. With a Corpus or Animal requisite, the spell can affect humans or animals. This causes +10 damage, against which armor is no defense, and makes the target extremely thirsty.

spells.name: Closing the Earth's Gate
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Perdo Aquam Spells > LEVEL 25 > Closing the Earth's Gate
spells.description: Dries a single spring, so that it never flows again. This spell targets the spring, not the water in it at a particular time, so the spring does not start to flow again later.

spells.name: Curse of the Desert
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Perdo Aquam Spells > Curse of the Desert
spells.description: Removes virtually all the fluid from the target's body, doing +15 damage, which armor does not protect against. The target must drink within a few minutes of being struck with this spell, or it dies. Casting requisites are Animal for beasts, and Corpus for humans.

spells.name: Calling the Odious Drought
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Year, T: Bound, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 5, +1 Touch, +4 Year, +4 Boundary, no increase for requisite
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Perdo Aquam Spells > LEVEL 50 > Calling the Odious Drought
spells.description: Causes a drought over the surrounding area. Only one-tenth the normal rain falls, streams dry up, and rivers dwindle.

spells.name: Ward against Faeries of the Waters
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Ring, T: Circle
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > GENERAL > Ward against Faeries of the Waters
spells.description: No water faerie whose Faerie Might is equal to or less than the level of the spell can affect those targeted by the spell. Faeries cannot directly or indirectly break the magic circle, nor can they use ranged attacks or magic to affect those within it. Seen from certain angles at night, the ring appears as a light blue dome.

spells.name: Cloak of the Duck's Feathers
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 1, +1 Touch, +2 Sun, +1 for slightly unnatural control
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Cloak of the Duck's Feathers
spells.description: Makes water run off one object or creature, protecting the target and the target's apparel from dampness. The spell is broken if the target is submerged in water.

spells.name: Break the Oncoming Wave
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 5, +1 Conc, ward, so the target is the warded Individual, not the water
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 10 > Break the Oncoming Wave
spells.description: Breaks all waves and torrents of water (including magical ones) just before they touch you. The water either crashes to the ground or flows around you in a truly impressive display. In Ferramentum of Verditius's version of this spell, the water breaks precisely one inch from the target, leaving a very clean line showing where the waves stopped. All of his spells may be recognized by their orderliness.

spells.name: Breath of Winter
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Breath of Winter
spells.description: Turns a circle of water up to five paces across into snow. The Duration ensures that it will not melt until the spell expires, no matter how warm the weather.

spells.name: Push of the Gentle Wave
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Push of the Gentle Wave
spells.description: A low wave is formed to propel a small boat slowly through the water. When casting this spell, you commonly make a pushing gesture. This spell, and other similar ones, pick out a particular current in the body of water and make it behave as desired, and thus have Individual Targets. In Silva of Merinita's version of this spell, the wave occasionally drifts or bobs about, as if it is happy and playful.

spells.name: Bridge of Frost
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Part
spells.design_notes: Base 3, +2 Voice, +2 Sun, +1 Part, +1 size, +1 to allow various shapes
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 30 > Bridge of Frost
spells.description: Causes a thick layer of frost (firm enough to walk on) to form on the surface of a body of water. The frost can take any shape up to fifteen paces in any direction. In Cralian of Tremere's version of this spell, the frost is so crystal clear that it can hardly be seen. All of his spells are very subtle.

spells.name: Chaos of the Angry Waves
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 30 > Chaos of the Angry Waves
spells.description: Causes water to churn wildly, overturning small water craft and forcing Swim rolls at -6, in a circular area 20 paces across.

spells.name: Parting the Waves
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 30 > Parting the Waves
spells.description: Parts a body of water, revealing a dry path 5 paces wide along the bottom. Very large bodies of water are too large for the Group target, and are thus unaffected.

spells.name: Waves of Drowning and Smashing
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 30 > Waves of Drowning and Smashing
spells.description: Raises a wave 5 feet high and 30 paces wide, which can submerge swimmers, overturn rowboats, and damage sailing ships. It starts as a small, curved wave and grows for the first 10 paces, at which point it reaches maximum size. If it travels 100 paces without striking an object large enough to break it up, it dissipates.

spells.name: Ice of Drowning
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Part
spells.design_notes: Base 5 (for the violent pounding), +2 Voice, +1 Concentration, +1 Part, +1 size, +1 additional effect, changing the water to ice
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 35 > Ice of Drowning
spells.description: Turns part of the water in a circle 10 paces across into large, jagged chunks of ice and drives them to pound against anything on the water's surface. The ice can punch holes in small boats but does not damage ships. Any swimmers in the area take +15 damage, and suffer -6 and two extra botch checks on Swim rolls.

spells.name: Pull of the Watery Grave
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 35 > Pull of the Watery Grave
spells.description: Creates a strong undertow that pulls any object smaller than a rowboat 25 fathoms (150 feet) into the depths. Each of those caught in the undercurrent must make a Strength + Swim stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 to avoid being dragged down. A roll is made each round that the spell is maintained.

spells.name: Tower of Whirling Water
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 35 > Tower of Whirling Water
spells.description: From a large body of water you form a waterspout that moves under your mental direction. It causes a simple die + 15 damage to anyone it hits (no Attack roll necessary). In addition, those struck must make a Quickness stress roll against an Ease Factor of 6 to avoid being sucked up by the waterspout. Those who fail are helplessly pulled into the spout and begin to drown immediately. When the waterspout fails, they fall up to 20 feet to the water's surface. If you direct the waterspout to move across land, you must make an Intelligence + Concentration stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 each round to maintain it.

spells.name: Neptune's Wrath
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Aquam Spells > Rego Aquam Spells > LEVEL 40 > Neptune's Wrath
spells.description:
A gigantic wave is created in a very large body of water. The wave, which is only 20 feet high but up to a mile wide, is capable of capsizing ships at sea, smashing and drowning people near shore, and seriously damaging coastline communities. The tidal wave needs 5 miles of water surface to build itself up to proper proportions. The magus cannot control the wave without casting another spell.

(Base 10, +3 Sight, +3 size, ritual for large effect)
---

spells.name: Air's Ghostly Form
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Diam, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 2, +1 Touch, +1 Diam, +1 unnatural (the spell can be cast indoors)
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 5 > Air's Ghostly Form
spells.description: A thick fog forms around the magus, extending outwards for several paces. A breeze can push the fog around. The fog dissipates at its natural rate, and may be too thin to restrict sight within a minute.

spells.name: Chamber of Spring Breezes
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 5 > Chamber of Spring Breezes
spells.description: Creates a breeze of fresh air that continually moves throughout a room, keeping the air continuously breathable even if the room is airtight. Rose of Jerbiton's version of this spell always causes the air to smell of roses. The breeze can be created outdoors, but there it has little effect.

spells.name: Jupiter's Resounding Blow
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 3, +2 Voice, +1 unnatural (the spell functions indoors)
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 10 > Jupiter's Resounding Blow
spells.description: Creates a thunderclap, anyone directly underneath must make a Stamina stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 or be deafened. If deafened, the target gets another Stamina simple roll each minute against the same Ease Factor and recovers on a success. If the first roll botches, the victim is rendered deaf for a month.

spells.name: Stench of the Twenty Corpses
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Diam, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 10 > Stench of the Twenty Corpses
spells.description: Creates a horrible stench of rotting corpses. All those within fifty paces of the target point must make Stamina stress rolls against an Ease Factor of 6 or act with a -3 penalty on all rolls. Anyone who botches the roll vomits and is incapacitated for (10 - Stamina) rounds.

spells.name: Wreaths of Foul Smoke
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Diam, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 10 > Wreaths of Foul Smoke
spells.description: Thick, yellow, sulfurous smoke rises up from the spot you designate, spreading and thinning naturally, but thick enough to block sight in a radius of about five paces around the point of origin. For each round anyone breathes the smoke, a Stamina stress roll against an Ease Factor of 3 must be made or a Fatigue level is lost. Once a character is Unconscious, further failed rolls result in the character taking a Light Wound each time. The area where the spell is cast will be damaged: small plants wilt and die, and tree growth is stunted. A faint stench of sulfur clings for days to anything exposed to the smoke.

spells.name: Broom of the Winds
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 15 > Broom of the Winds
spells.description: Whips up violent, swirling winds around the target. The target must make a Size stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 to remain standing. If the target is braced or holding onto some support, he may also make a Strength stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9. If both rolls fail, the target is knocked in a random direction. The target may be damaged, depending on whether he strikes anything.

spells.name: Charge of the Angry Winds
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 15 > Charge of the Angry Winds
spells.description: A wall of wind roars away from you, starting up to 10 paces away and continuing until it dissipates naturally. The wind is confined by any existing barriers, but if unconfined it can be up to a hundred paces wide. All within the area must make a Dexterity + Size stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 or fall down and be blown along by the winds. The rolls must be made at the start of the gale and each subsequent round that the wind is maintained. You must concentrate on the gale. Missile fire into or out of the gale is futile, and marching against the gale requires a Strength + Size stress roll against an Ease Factor of 15. Failure in this attempt mandates another Dexterity + Size stress roll against an Ease Factor of 12 to keep from falling.

spells.name: Circling Winds of Protection
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 3, +1 Touch, +1 Conc, +2 unnatural, +1 Rego effect
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 20 > Circling Winds of Protection
spells.description: Surrounds you with winds that circle at great speed. Since the wind picks up dust and other small, loose objects, you may be obscured. Anyone standing near enough to attack you with a hand weapon must make a Size stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 at the beginning of each round or be blown back. Melee Attack rolls against you are at –3, and missile or thrown attacks are at –9.

spells.name: Clouds of Rain and Thunder
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Conc, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 25 > Clouds of Rain and Thunder
spells.description: Creates a small but severe rainstorm, including lightning and winds. The storm gathers and breaks in a few seconds as the caster concentrates, and dissipates with similar speed when he stops doing so. Note that reducing the Range for this spell makes the storm increasingly unnatural, so versions of this spell with lower Range still have the same level.

spells.name: Clouds of Summer Snow
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Conc, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 25 > Clouds of Summer Snow
spells.description: Creates clouds that drop snow over an area about half a mile across. The clouds gather over a few seconds at the beginning of the spell, and dissipate over the same sort of time when the caster stops concentrating. The spell does not affect temperature.

spells.name: Pull of the Skybound Winds
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 30 > Pull of the Skybound Winds
spells.description: Makes winds rise upward, pulling one object, creature, or person up to 50 feet into the sky before letting that target drop. Even a small building can be torn from its foundation, though such a building can be no larger than 20 feet to a side, and cannot be built out of a material heavier than stout wood. Uprooted objects fall randomly, unless you make a Finesse stress roll against an Ease Factor of 12 to choose where the objects will fall.

spells.name: Wings of the Soaring Wind
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 5, +1 Touch, +1 Conc, +2, highly unnatural, +1 Rego requisite
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 30 > Wings of the Soaring Wind
spells.description: Generates a massive gust of air around you that supports and pushes you along through the air at speeds up to 40 miles per hour. Hovering in place is difficult, and requires a Finesse roll against an Ease Factor of 9 per round. It is dangerous for extended travel, because if you lose concentration, you are likely to sustain serious injuries (+15 damage, on average, although it may be more if the magus was very high up).

spells.name: The Incantation of Lightning
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 35 > The Incantation of Lightning
spells.description: A lightning bolt shoots forth from your outstretched hand in the direction you are pointing, doing +30 damage to a single target it hits. There must be nothing between you and the target. Those near the target must make Size stress rolls against an Ease Factor of 6 to remain standing.

spells.name: Breath of the Open Sky
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 5, +1 Touch, +1 Conc, +4 size, +1 unnatural, ritual because of spectacular effect
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 40 > Breath of the Open Sky
spells.description: You can call up a wind capable of devastating the countryside. You must stand under the open sky to cast this spell. While you cast the spell, a breeze picks up, blowing from you in the direction you are facing. When the ritual is completed, the wind strengthens to a gale roaring over the visible countryside and beyond, strong enough to topple wooden structures and knock down trees. The wind does not weaken while it is within your sight, but loses its force naturally where you cannot see it. If you turn away from your original facing for more than a few seconds, you have to make a Concentration roll against an Ease Factor of 12 to maintain the spell. If you turn away for a minute or more, the spell ends. The air behind you is still.

spells.name: Wrath of Whirling Winds and Water
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Sun, T: Group, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Creo Auram Spells > LEVEL 65 > Wrath of Whirling Winds and Water
spells.description: This spell creates an immense storm. 40 or more miles wide, of hurricane intensity. The storm builds up while you cast the ritual, and dissipates in a few seconds when the spell's duration ends. The gale-force winds and lashing waves cause immense damage along shorelines, and the winds, rain, and lightning cause extensive damage inland as well. Visibility is reduced to a few paces, missile fire is futile, and travel is nearly suicidal. Weak structures are blown over, and all but the most seaworthy ships are certain to founder unless they can be beached before the storm reaches full intensity. The storm is not under the caster's control, and moves normally.

spells.name: Sailor's Foretaste of the Morrow
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom. T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Intellego Auram Spells > LEVEL 15 > Sailor's Foretaste of the Morrow
spells.description: You know precisely what tomorrow's weather will be, and you gain a general impression about the weather for the week ahead. This intuition only applies to the place where you cast the spell, and is based on extrapolating current conditions, so it is occasionally wrong (precise, but inaccurate). Still, it would take something like an interfering magical being, or a volcanic eruption, to throw the prediction off.

spells.name: True Sight of the Air
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Vision
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Intellego Auram Spells > LEVEL 15 > True Sight of the Air
spells.description: Lets you see clearly through all manner of obfuscation in the air nearby, including smoke, fog, and dust, even if the obfuscation is magical.

spells.name: Whispering Winds
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Intellego Auram Spells > LEVEL 15 > Whispering Winds
spells.description: The winds bear their tidings to your ears, allowing you to hear words spoken by any group of people within your line of sight, provided no solid barrier (including glass) intervenes. This spell is an adaptation of an effect known to Bjornaer the Founder, and fits poorly into the normal framework of Hermetic magic.

spells.name: Eyes of the Bat
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Hearing
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Intellego Auram Spells > LEVEL 25 > Eyes of the Bat
spells.description: You can move about confidently in complete darkness by sensing air and its boundaries (for example, where solid objects are). You 'hear' the boundaries of the air, so it does not permit fine discriminations. On the other hand, you can 'hear' boundaries behind you.

spells.name: Rain of Stones
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Muto Auram Spells > LEVEL 20 > Rain of Stones
spells.description:
Reg: Terram

Turns rain into stones as it falls, causing +0 damage every two rounds (they're small stones), and maybe less if the rain is very light. People under cover are not affected. The stones revert to water when the spell expires. If it stops raining before the spell ends, no more rain is transformed, even if a different rain storm starts.

spells.name: Talons of the Winds
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Diam, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 4, +2 Voice, +1 Diam, +1 Rego requisite (additional effect: protect the caster)
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Muto Auram Spells > LEVEL 20 > Talons of the Winds
spells.description: Transforms a wind into an abrasive medium that tears and claws at everything in its path. Soft materials like leaves and cloth are shredded, and people take +4 damage from innumerable bloody cuts and abrasions. This spell cannot be cast if there is not a breeze of some sort blowing, thus making it difficult to use effectively indoors. The caster is not affected by the spell, even if the wind is blowing directly at him. In Ossium of Bjornaer's version of this spell a strange keening can be heard in the air as it cuts through targets. Ossium's wizard's sigil manifests by making many of his spells eerie or frightening.

spells.name: Infernal Smoke of Death
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Muto Auram Spells > LEVEL 40 > Infernal Smoke of Death
spells.description: Turns normal smoke from a fire into a thick, corrosive cloud. Anyone immersed in the cloud of smoke takes +25 corrosion damage, against which armor provides only half its normal Protection value. Soft materials like cloth, fur, paper, and leather are charred and ruined by exposure to the noxious vapor. While you concentrate, any new smoke becomes corrosive. The smoke is heavier than air, so rolls along the ground, pooling in low spots. The damage represents normal exposure to the vapor, and may be modified if characters spend unusually short or long times within it.

spells.name: Fog of Confusion
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Year, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 2, +1 Touch, +4 Year, +4 Size, +1 Imaginem requisite, +1 Rego requisite
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Muto Auram Spells > LEVEL 45 > Fog of Confusion
spells.description: Turns a bank of fog up to six miles across into a silvery mist too thick to see through. Random screams, thumps, hisses, and other noises harass and confuse anyone within the fog, perhaps even leading them into danger. People have extreme difficulty navigating through the fog, especially through unknown territory. No more than seven people, who must be present at the ritual, can see normally through the fog. The fog does not dissipate until the spell expires.

spells.name: Rain of Oil
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Sun, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Muto Auram Spells > LEVEL 50 > Rain of Oil
spells.description: Turns rain into droplets of oil. Within the spell's duration, the rain of oil continues as long as the storm does, drenching the landscape with flammable liquid. The oil will evaporate at a natural rate after the rain ceases. A Creo Ignem spell can ignite the storm so that the oil burns as it falls, but this requires a Group target large enough to catch all the raindrops. Lighting the oil after it has landed is much easier.

spells.name: Room of Stale Air
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Room
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Perdo Auram Spells > LEVEL 15 > Room of Stale Air
spells.description: Makes the air in a room stuffy and uncomfortable. Any creatures that breathe air suffer a –3 penalty on all rolls involving physical exertion. In addition, for each round of exertion a character must make a simple Stamina roll against an Ease Factor of 6 or lose a Fatigue level. This may cause panic in some creatures or people. The speed at which the air refreshes itself depends on how well ventilated the room is.

spells.name: Quiet the Raging Winds
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Perdo Auram Spells > LEVEL 20 > Quiet the Raging Winds
spells.description: Stops all wind from blowing, leaving only calm air. This spell is not powerful enough to affect winds caused by Ritual magic, and only affects winds blowing on the caster when it is cast. Under normal circumstances, the winds will not pick up again for at least an hour or so.

spells.name: The Cloudless Sky Returned
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Mom, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Perdo Auram Spells > LEVEL 45 > The Cloudless Sky Returned
spells.description: This spell clears the sky within your vision of any clouds or other meteorological activity short of a hurricane or tornado, either naturally occurring or caused by magic. The clouds take a few seconds to clear, during which time you must concentrate on them. This spell only affects meteorological activity in progress at the moment of casting, and is not powerful enough to dispel a weather condition caused by Ritual magic. In Ferramentum's version of this spell, the clouds clear from the sky in an orderly manner, as if they were soldiers marching on parade.

spells.name: Ward against Faeries of the Air
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Ring, T: Circle
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Rego Auram Spells > GENERAL > Ward against Faeries of the Air
spells.description: As Ward Against Faeries of the Water (ReAq Gen), but for faeries of the air. The faerie cannot directly or indirectly break the magic circle, nor can they use ranged attacks or magic to affect those within it. From some angles at night, the ring may be seen as a violethued dome.

spells.name: Wind at the Back
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Spec, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Rego Auram Spells > LEVEL 5 > Wind at the Back
spells.description: An existing breeze follows you until you stop traveling for more than ten minutes. It causes phenomena you pass through (fog, haze) to follow you.

spells.name: Ward against Rain
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Rego Auram Spells > LEVEL 10 > Ward against Rain
spells.description: No rain falls on the caster, no matter how bad the weather.

spells.name: Clouds of Thunderous Might
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Conc, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Rego Auram Spells > LEVEL 30 > Clouds of Thunderous Might
spells.description: Gathers pre-existing clouds and winds into a small storm. The storm gathers at a normal speed, but is under the caster's control as long as he can see it. The caster may use the control granted by this spell to dissipate small storms as well. If the caster does not dissipate the storm before stopping concentrating, the storm continues as normal.

spells.name: Gathering of the Stormy Might
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Conc, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Auram Spells > Rego Auram Spells > LEVEL 40 > Gathering of the Stormy Might
spells.description:
As for Clouds of Thunderous Might, but the storm is larger, possibly covering the entire visible sky. Again, it is under the caster's control.

(Base 4, +3 Sight, +1 Conc, +2 Group, +2 size)
---

spells.name: Bind Wound
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 10 > Bind Wound
spells.description: This spell binds the target's wounds, so that he can undertake any activity without the risk of worsening the wounds. He still suffers from the wound penalties, however, and cannot heal naturally while under the influence of this spell. Typically, you place your hands on the target and pass them over his wounds, which magically seal themselves and stop bleeding.

spells.name: Charm against Putrefaction
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Moon, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 10 > Charm against Putrefaction
spells.description: Prevents decay of a human corpse, or of a severed body part. Necromancers use it to preserve their revived corpses.

spells.name: The Chirurgeon's Healing Touch
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 20 > The Chirurgeon's Healing Touch
spells.description: This spell heals a single Light Wound suffered by the person touched. This spell does not heal damage from poison or disease.

spells.name: Gentle Touch of the Purified Body
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 20 > Gentle Touch of the Purified Body
spells.description: The target is cured of a Light Wound caused by poison or disease. This spell does not heal damage caused by injury.

spells.name: Purification of the Festering Wounds
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Moon, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 20 > Purification of the Festering Wounds
spells.description: The target gains a +9 bonus to Recovery rolls, as long as he has been under the influence of this spell for the whole of the recovery interval. The recovery interval is counted from the time that the spell is cast, any previous time is ignored.

spells.name: Restoration of the Defiled Body
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 25 > Restoration of the Defiled Body
spells.description: Removes the crippling or malignant after-effects of any injury, poison, or disease, or of premature aging. The effects of natural aging cannot be undone by this or any spell. This spell does not heal wounds, only extra effects.

spells.name: The Severed Limb Made Whole
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 25, +1 Touch, -1 because the old limb is needed
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 25 > The Severed Limb Made Whole
spells.description: A limb that has been detached from the body can be reattached, but any decay that has occurred on the severed limb remains after the limb is reattached. After a day of decomposition, a limb is weak. After seven days, it is nearly useless and infects the character on whom it is replaced (Stamina stress roll against an Ease Factor of 6 or take a Medium Wound). If it's been severed for more than two weeks, the limb is completely useless.

spells.name: Cheating the Reaper
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 30 > Cheating the Reaper
spells.description: Resolves a major (or less severe) crisis caused by aging. The target still requires rest for the remainder of the season to recover fully. Note that each application of this spell causes Warping. See page 392 for details on crises.

spells.name: Incantation of the Body Made Whole
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 40 > Incantation of the Body Made Whole
spells.description: Heals all damage to a human body at the conclusion of the ritual. The spell can only heal wounds, not such damage as missing limbs, or diseases and damage from poisons.

spells.name: The Shadow of Life Renewed
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 70, +1 Touch, no addition for requisite
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Creo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 75 > The Shadow of Life Renewed
spells.description:
Brings the semblance of life to a corpse. During the ritual, healing spells work on the corpse, which is important because the body must be physically whole or else it simply dies again once it is brought back to life. For each day that the person has been dead, roll for aging. When the ritual is complete, roll a simple die to determine the success of the attempt:
| Roll | Result |
| ---- | ------ |
| 1    | Body dissolves  |
| 2    | Body possessed by a demon, faerie, or other supernatural entity |
| 3    | Mindless, useless living corpse |
| 4–5  | Automaton with no Abilities over 3 |
| 6–7  | Automaton, but Abilities as a normal person's |
| 8    | Person with self direction but no personality, and somehow dangerously demented |
| 9–10 | Person with will, but no personality. The revived person is not truly alive and cannot gain experience points. Must make an aging roll at the beginning of each year of existence, regardless of age. This is the closest the Order has gotten to raising the dead. |

spells.name: Sight of the True Form
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Mom, T: Vision
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Intellego Corpus Spells > GENERAL > Sight of the True Form
spells.description: You see the true, original form of any person whose form has been changed or masked. This spell can see through mundane masks and disguises at level 10, and can see through the effects of other spells that are equal to or lower than the level of this spell.

spells.name: Physician's Eye
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Intellego Corpus Spells > LEVEL 5 > Physician's Eye
spells.description: Determines the general health of a single person. Specific afflictions appear to you as areas of yellow coloration on the person's body. A Perception + Medicine stress roll is required to identify unusual diseases. The Ease Factor is determined by the rarity of the disease.

spells.name: Revealed Flaws of Mortal Flesh
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Intellego Corpus Spells > LEVEL 10 > Revealed Flaws of Mortal Flesh
spells.description: You are able to find any medical defects in a person or being that you are touching. This provides more, and more specific, information than Physician's Eye.

spells.name: Whispers through the Black Gate
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc. T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 5, +1 Touch, +1 Conc, no cost for requisite
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Intellego Corpus Spells > LEVEL 15 > Whispers through the Black Gate
spells.description: You can speak through the barrier — the figurative "gate" — that stands between the dead and the living, to one corpse that has not yet decayed into a skeleton. The corpse cannot have been buried in Church burial, nor have belonged to a spirit that went straight to Heaven (for example, a saint or a crusader). The spirit that you speak with is not compelled to tell the truth; you can, of course, find ways to coerce or trick it into doing so. All those around you can hear the voice of the corpse.

spells.name: The Inexorable Search
spells.parameter_line: R: Arc, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Intellego Corpus Spells > LEVEL 20 > The Inexorable Search
spells.description: Determines the location of a specific person. To cast the spell you need a map and an Arcane Connection. After casting the spell, you can move your finger over the map at the rate of one hour per square foot of map. When your finger passes over the person's location as represented on the map, you sense the person's presence. (If the person is not in the area covered by the map, no sensations result.) You can locate the person to within a thumb's width on the map. A similar spell allows you to search for a dead body (Tracing the Trail of Death's Stench).

spells.name: The Eye of the Sage
spells.parameter_line: R: Arc, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Intellego Corpus Spells > LEVEL 30 > The Eye of the Sage
spells.description: Lets you see a specific person and what is within one pace of that person. The image is clear enough to allow reading.

spells.name: Eyes of the Cat
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 5 > Eyes of the Cat
spells.description: The target gains the eyes of a cat, which allow him or her to see in near darkness (but not in absolute darkness, such as a lightless subterranean cavern).

spells.name: Disguise of the New Visage
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 15 > Disguise of the New Visage
spells.description: The target's facial features are transformed to any approximately human configuration you choose. In Cralian of Tremere's version of this spell, the new visage is always as unassuming as possible.

spells.name: Preternatural Growth and Shrinking
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 3, +1 Touch, +2 Sun, +1 because the spell allows growth or two kinds of shrinking
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 15 > Preternatural Growth and Shrinking
spells.description: Adds +1 to the target's normal Size or decreases it by up to 2 points.

spells.name: Arm of the Infant
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 20 > Arm of the Infant
spells.description: Shrinks a person's arm to half its original length and makes it pudgy, like an infant's.

spells.name: Gift of the Bear's Fortitude
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 25 > Gift of the Bear's Fortitude
spells.description: Your flesh becomes resistant to physical damage. You get +3 to your Soak. Your flesh is tough and insensitive; any rolls that involve a sensitive touch (such as for picking a lock) are at -1.

spells.name: Shape of the Woodland Prowler
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 25 > Shape of the Woodland Prowler
spells.description: You place a wolf skin over yourself or another, and the target changes into a wolf. The target may change back at will, ending the spell. Before casting this spell, you must first, of course, acquire the skin of a wolf, if you want to be able to end the spell early.

spells.name: Cloak of Black Feathers
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 30 > Cloak of Black Feathers
spells.description: You hang a small cape of raven feathers on your back and transform into a raven. You can resume human form at will, but doing so ends the spell.

spells.name: Curse of Circe
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 30 > Curse of Circe
spells.description: Turns a person into a pig.

spells.name: Stance of the Patient Tree
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 35 > Stance of the Patient Tree
spells.description: You turn yourself into a tree about 12 feet high, with a trunk about a foot thick. You can sense only the most basic things, such as night and day, strong winds, and things that can affect a tree. You can resume human form at will, but doing so ends the spell. While in tree form, you do not heal any wounds or restore any Fatigue levels. The tree branch you use to let yourself change back determines the kind of tree you become, and you cannot cast the spell without the branch.

spells.name: Cloak of Mist
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 40 > Cloak of Mist
spells.description: You turn yourself into a thick, cohesive mist of approximately human size. You can float through the air at walking speed and can see and hear what is going on around you. You can also seep through cracks, but cannot move through solid objects. You may return to human form at any time, but doing so ends the spell. As mist, you are subject to the forces of the wind. In order to cast this spell, you inhale some mist, and you keep the mist in your 'mouth' until you want to end the spell.

spells.name: Transform to Water
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 40 > Transform to Water
spells.description: You turn yourself into water, one pint for every pound of the your weight. As water, you can hold yourself together unless someone makes an effort to separate part of you from the rest. You can roll slowly across the ground, but cannot move uphill. You can hear sounds, feel things that touch you, and can sense temperature. You can resume human form at will, but doing so ends the spell. You cannot resume human form if a significant part of your body/water is separated from the rest. You pour a small amount of water into your hands when you cast the spell.

spells.name: The Silent Vigil
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Moon, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 45 > The Silent Vigil
spells.description: You can move into and become part of a rock formation that is at least twice your size. While in the stone, you can hear what is going on around you, but you cannot use other senses. The spell lasts until you come out, and you must come out where you went in. You have no need for food or drink, though you do age. Rolls to recover from Fatigue or wounds take twice as long to make while you are in the stone. Casting requisites are required for items you take with you into the stone.

spells.name: Mists of Change
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun & Year, T: Bound, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 10, +2 Voice, +4 Year, +4 Bound, slightly nonstandard effect, mist and wind are cosmetic only
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Muto Corpus Spells > LEVEL 60 > Mists of Change
spells.description:
As this spell is cast, a whipping wind arises and patches of mist begin to roll across the landscape. Sometimes, a patch of mist suddenly envelops a person for a few seconds, during which time the victim cannot move. When the mist blows away, continuing its course, the person is left transformed into a random creature. Roll a simple die to determine the result:
| Roll | Animal                             |
| ---- | ---------------------------------- |
| 1    | Wolf                               |
| 2    | Horse                              |
| 3    | Brown Bear                         |
| 4    | Large Toad                         |
| 5    | Ferret                             |
| 6    | Viper                              |
| 7    | Boar/Sow                           |
| 8    | Dog                                |
| 9    | Cat                                |
| 0    | Other (storyguide picks; be cruel) |
People transformed retain their human minds, but inherit all the passions and instincts of their new forms. The mist covers the target area, which must be in sight when you cast the spell. The mists affect the area until sunrise or sunset, and transform anyone who remains in the area during that time. For important characters, roll a simple die when the spell is cast and every half hour afterwards, and on a roll lower than the number of rolls made so far the mists envelope and transform them at some point in the next half hour. The transformations last for a Year.

spells.name: Touch of the Goose Feather
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 5 > Touch of the Goose Feather
spells.description: The target lets out a loud sneeze. Spellcasters who sneeze must make Intelligence + Concentration stress rolls against an Ease Factor of 12 to maintain concentration, and others get –1 on most activities at the moment of the sneeze.

spells.name: Dust to Dust
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 15 > Dust to Dust
spells.description: Turns a dead body or mindless undead body to dust in two rounds. The spell doesn't affect those undead possessed by spirits. To do this to an animal corpse requires a Perdo Animal spell.

spells.name: The Wound That Weeps
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > The Wound That Weeps
spells.description: When casting this spell, you point at the victim and a large wound opens on his or her body. The wound is not a deep one, but it bleeds profusely. This is only a Light Wound, but it looks bad.

spells.name: Invocation of Weariness
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 20 > Invocation of Weariness
spells.description: The target loses a Fatigue level. The phrases used with this spell are usually singsong melodies, which lull the victim into drowsiness.

spells.name: Bane of the Decrepit Body
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom. T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 25 > Bane of the Decrepit Body
spells.description: The target ages 5 years. Determine effects normally. This spell does not work on children younger than age 16.

spells.name: Curse of the Leprous Flesh
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 25 > Curse of the Leprous Flesh
spells.description: This spell inflicts leprosy on the target, which causes a person's flesh to rot off in a matter of weeks a truly hideous sight. Much of the target's flesh literally hangs from his or her frame, and there is a pungent rotting smell about him at all times. The target takes an Incapacitating Wound from the disease, and must roll as normal to recover. The target also loses a point of Stamina by the time the disease ends. If the victim somehow survives this curse, he is shunned by mundane communities. If the victim succeeds in a Stamina roll against an Ease Factor of 6 when the spell is cast, he shakes off the disease, although he still feels unwell for a few days, and may have problems if an astute doctor realizes he is leprous.

spells.name: Grip of the Choking Hand
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 25 > Grip of the Choking Hand
spells.description: The target feels an invisible, strangling hand on the throat. Each round, the target loses a Fatigue level. Once the target falls unconscious, the spell has no further effect. The spell works by seriously constricting the target's windpipe, hence the feeling. This constriction is not quite enough to kill, however.

spells.name: Incantation of the Milky Eyes
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 30 > Incantation of the Milky Eyes
spells.description: Blinds a target, leaving the eyes milky with cataracts. This heals as a Heavy Wound.

spells.name: Twist of the Tongue
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Part
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > Twist of the Tongue
spells.description: Twists the target's tongue into a spiral, completely destroying the ability to speak. A grave offense to a magus. The damage heals as a Medium Wound.

spells.name: Clenching Grasp of the Crushed Heart
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 40 > Clenching Grasp of the Crushed Heart
spells.description: When casting this spell, you make a clenching motion with your hand. The victim doubles over and dies at once.

spells.name: The Kiss of Death
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 45 > The Kiss of Death
spells.description: The person you kiss dies a sudden death. The only mark on the corpse is the black imprint of your lips. You do not need to speak when casting this spell, and thus gain no penalty from silence. (This is why the spell is higher level than the guidelines would indicate.)

spells.name: Curse of the Unportended Plague
spells.parameter_line: R: Sight, D: Mom, T: Bound, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Corpus Spells > Perdo Corpus Spells > LEVEL 55 > Curse of the Unportended Plague
spells.description: Starts a plague in a city or other Boundary. Everyone within the boundary is afflicted with the disease when the ritual is complete, and suffers as described under Curse of the Leprous Flesh, on the previous page. The conditions causing the disease last but a moment, so people entering the area after the spell is cast are safe. Using, or even transcribing, this spell is rigorously discouraged by most of the Order of Hermes. (Note: the disease caused by this spell is not the later Bubonic Plague, which is unknown in Europe in 1220.)

spells.name: Conjure the Sturdy Vine
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Creo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Conjure the Sturdy Vine
spells.description: Grows 10 paces of vine from a moderate quantity of wood, or from fertile soil. The vine is extremely strong and pliable, suitable for use as rope.

spells.name: Trap of the Entwining Vines
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Creo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Trap of the Entwining Vines
spells.description: Causes strong, woody vines to grow rapidly from fertile earth. The vines cover a circle 2 paces across and reach 6 feet high. Anything caught within them is immobilized. To avoid the vines, the target must make a Quickness — Encumbrance stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9. To break out requires a Strength stress roll against an Ease Factor of 12 (allowed once a round). Someone not trapped can cut a trapped person free in two rounds, assuming proper tools. The vines weaken and wither to dust at sunrise or sunset.

spells.name: Bridge of Wood
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Creo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Bridge of Wood
spells.description: Creates a bridge made from living leaves, vines, and wood. The bridge can reach up to 20 paces, and is five paces wide. It is sturdy enough to support creatures of up to Size +4.

spells.name: Wall of Thorns
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Creo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Wall of Thorns
spells.description: Within seconds, this spell creates a straight wall of woody, thorny bushes up to 20 paces long, 1 pace thick, and 4 paces high. The thorns, which are unnaturally resilient, have a +15 Soak, are cut by edged weapons only, and cutting through requires inflicting a Heavy Wound. Bodily forcing one's way through the wall requires a Strength stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 and does +15 damage whether the attempt succeeds or fails. If the roll botches, an additional 5 points of damage are suffered. The wall grows out of existing bushes or fertile soil. A Dexterity + Athletics stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9 allows a character to scale the wall, but the character takes +10 damage in the process.

spells.name: Wall of Living Wood
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Creo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 25 > Wall of Living Wood
spells.description: A straight wall of living oak is created, growing quickly from nearby trees, other plant growth, or fertile earth. The wall is 20 paces long, 1 pace thick, and 5 paces high. It can be scaled with a Dexterity + Athletics stress roll against an Ease Factor of 9.

spells.name: The Bountiful Feast
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Year, T: Special, Ritual
spells.design_notes: Base 1, +1 Touch, +4 Year, +4 Special (equivalent to Boundary), +1 Size (for a total of +4 Size, including the +3 from the guideline, so that the area affected is up to about 6 miles across
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Creo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 35 > The Bountiful Feast
spells.description: This ritual, which must be cast at noon on the day after the winter solstice, attempts to ensure that all crops that grow within its target will be healthy and fruitful. The spell protects against non-magical blight and other diseases. It can't control the weather or prevent others from harming crops, but it does cause crops to be healthier, larger, and tastier than they otherwise would be. The target of this spell is the fields owned by those present at the ritual. The spell must be cast within the region affected by the spell, although that region need not be contiguous. Although food plants are affected most by this ritual, all plants are made healthier to some degree.

spells.name: Probe Nature's Hidden Lore
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Intellego Herbam Spells > LEVEL 4 > Probe Nature's Hidden Lore
spells.description: Tells you what kind of plant you are touching. You also get an image of it in its natural habitat. Works with plant products as well as with plants themselves.

spells.name: Hunt for the Wild Herb
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Conc, T: Smell
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Intellego Herbam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Hunt for the Wild Herb
spells.description: When you cast this spell, you can smell one sort of plant, and follow the scent, as long as you continue to concentrate. You must have a sample of the type of plant you are looking for. A Perception stress roll against an Ease Factor of 6 is needed to follow the scent. If there is no plant of the relevant type fairly close by (close enough to smell), you smell nothing, although the spell still works.

spells.name: Intuition of the Forest
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Intellego Herbam Spells > LEVEL 10 > Intuition of the Forest
spells.description: You engage in a type of communion with the woods that you are in, giving you an intuitive sense of how to get along in the forest, and how to get the most out of the forest without causing it harm. You get a +3 to all nature-oriented rolls (not including spells) when in a forest or similar area. Causing significant harm to the forest cancels this spell.

spells.name: Shriek of the Impending Shafts
spells.parameter_line: R: Per, D: Sun, T: Hearing
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Intellego Herbam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Shriek of the Impending Shafts
spells.description: Lets you know where anything wooden will be in the immediate future by a shriek that sounds in the air in advance of the wood's coming. A piece of wood that is controlled may change course, so the spell is sometimes wrong. You get a +9 bonus on Defense scores against wooden weapons (provided that you have the freedom to dodge), and you can automatically dodge wooden missiles fired from more than ten paces away. The shriek is only audible to you, and it cannot be simulated by people making noises.

spells.name: Converse with Plant and Tree
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Conc, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Herbam Spells > Intellego Herbam Spells > LEVEL 25 > Converse with Plant and Tree
spells.description: You can speak with a plant for one conversation. The level of conversation depends on the type of plants; longer-lived and more noble plants have more to say. A single question and response takes ten to thirty minutes, with slower-growing plants speaking more slowly. Typically, plants can talk about the soil and about other plants around them, but have limited awareness of more momentary events, such as the passing of animals or people, unless that passing has a direct bearing on the plant (for example, a person cut its trunk). News can spread quickly through a forest when it has to, called from tree to tree on the wind. Bystanders cannot understand your conversation.

spells.name: Pass the Unyielding Portal
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Diam, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Muto Herbam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Pass the Unyielding Portal
spells.description: Makes the wood of a door pliable, allowing the magus to bend it open regardless of most locks or bars.

spells.name: Transformation of the Thorny Staff
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Muto Herbam Spells > LEVEL 10 > Transformation of the Thorny Staff
spells.description: Causes a length of wood (up to 6 feet in length) to grow thick, sharp thorns at whatever spots you touch. If the spell is cast on a staff, the thorns add +4 to the damage done by the staff.

spells.name: Piercing Shaft of Wood
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Muto Herbam Spells > LEVEL 10 > Piercing Shaft of Wood
spells.description: Turns a piece of wood into a sleek, barbed, strong shaft that flies through the air at a target. Damage depends on the size of the item from which the shaft is formed: staff (upper size limit) +10, 2-foot branch +8, wand +5. This spell cannot miss, but it is resisted.

spells.name: Rope of Bronze
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.design_notes: Base 4, +1 Touch, +2 Sun, no cost for requisite
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Muto Herbam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Rope of Bronze
spells.description: Turns a piece of rope (made from plant material) into refined, superior bronze, which does not break under normal circumstances.

spells.name: Thaumaturgical Transformation of Plants to Iron
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind Reg: Terram
spells.design_notes: Base 4, +1 Touch, +2 Sun, +1 size, no cost for requisite
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Muto Herbam Spells > LEVEL 20 > Thaumaturgical Transformation of Plants to Iron
spells.description: Makes any plant, dead or alive, as hard as iron. Affects any amount of continuous plant material up to the size of a small tree. Sharp-edged plants can become deadly when they are made this hard.

spells.name: Stir the Slumbering Tree
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Sun, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Muto Herbam Spells > LEVEL 25 > Stir the Slumbering Tree
spells.description: Raises any tree into a state of consciousness resembling that of a human. Such a tree can see and hear what is going on around it, and at a normal human level of perception. Such a tree may share some mental attributes or personality traits with the caster. The tree can rearrange its branches over the course of several hours, though too slowly for people to observe the movement from second to second. Most trees in magical areas are already awake.

spells.name: Curse of the Haunted Forest
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Year, T: Group, Ritual
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Muto Herbam Spells > LEVEL 60 > Curse of the Haunted Forest
spells.description: Turns a previously normal small forest into a haunted and dangerous place. The trees become gnarled and hideous, and the imaginative can see menacing faces in them. The trees also become conscious and cruel; capable of sight, hearing, and limited movement. While they cannot uproot themselves, they can move their branches about, and can reach the ground in an hour or two. Only ill-tempered animals, such as boars and crows, live in these woods, and nothing in them is edible. The only knowledge of what might happen should this ritual be attempted in a faerie forest comes from ancient and disputed legends of the Order of Hermes.

spells.name: Curse of the Rotted Wood
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Perdo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 5 > Curse of the Rotted Wood
spells.description: Causes a non-living wooden object to rot and fall apart. Objects up to the size of a single door can be affected, but things that size take up to two rounds to decay. Usually, large chunks of wood start falling off the target, and slowly crumble to smaller and smaller pieces, until only dust is left. Ferramentum of Verditius's version of this spell is very orderly as per his wizard's sigil. The decay of the object starts from the top and continues to the bottom, leaving only dust as it goes.

spells.name: Wizard's Autumn
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Perdo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 15 > Wizard's Autumn
spells.description: All the deciduous leaves (that is, those that normally fall in autumn) on the target plants turn brown and then fall to the ground. The full process takes a minute. This spell can affect a small grove of trees. The trees do not bud again until the next spring.

spells.name: Plant's Withering Bane
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Ind
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Perdo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 20 > Plant's Withering Bane
spells.description: Withers and destroys one living plant, up to and including a plant the size of a small tree.

spells.name: The Great Rot
spells.parameter_line: R: Voice, D: Mom, T: Group
spells.heading_path: Chapter 9: Spells > Muto Herbam Guidelines > Perdo Herbam Spells > LEVEL 25 > The Great Rot
spells.description: Rots and destroys a large amount of dead wood, up to the amount found in a wooden house or small inn. Wooden structures creak and groan mightily for about a minute before collapsing.

spells.name: Treading the Ashen Path
spells.parameter_line: R: Touch, D: Mom, T: Group
